productionGatePolicy.summary: Production publish is blocked when a tracked security issue is open and not explicitly acknowledged, or when a tracked issue is marked resolved but is still observed in the production artifact.
productionGatePolicy.betaBehavior: Beta publish shows tracked issues as reminders only.

issues.title: Production public artifact exposes reference audio assets
issues.summary: The public hosted production site served assets/reference-audio clips directly. If those clips are intended as private/local reference material, production is leaking protected review assets.
evidence: All 24 referenced public reference-audio paths checked during the passive review returned HTTP 200.
proposedPlan: Confirm which reference-audio clips are private, licensed, or production-safe.
Remove private reference-audio files from production and beta public artifacts.
Keep local/dev reference-audio provisioning through ignored local assets or private artifact bootstrap only.
Add artifact-boundary checks that fail production if dist/production references or contains private reference-audio paths.

issues.title: Production bundle exposes harness and debug mutation APIs
issues.summary: The public production bundle exposes harness/debug globals capable of mutating gameplay state, clock control, scoring paths, replay state, audio capture, and leaderboard test state.
evidence: Public bundle includes window.__galagaHarness__ and aliases it to window.__platinumHarness__.
Harness methods include stage start, controlled clock advancement, score awarding, forced game over, remote score test setup, local replay seeding, and debug state reads.
Production bundle also contains force-write harness flag names.
proposedPlan: Strip harness/debug APIs from production builds by default.
Keep harness exports only in dev/test lanes, or guard them behind a compile-time production false constant.
Add production artifact checks for window.__galagaHarness__, window.__platinumHarness__, and force-write harness flag names.
Move diagnostic state needed by public support into narrow read-only reports.

issues.title: Leaderboard write integrity depends on client-trusted score submission
issues.summary: Score submission is assembled in the browser and sent through the public Supabase client. The anon key is expected to be public, but leaderboard integrity depends on strict RLS and server-side validation that the public artifact does not prove.
evidence: Public bundle includes SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY.
Public bundle inserts into the scores table from client-side score/game-over state.
Read-only passive checks confirmed scores and profiles are publicly selectable with the anon key.
No service-role key was observed in the public bundle.
proposedPlan: Audit Supabase RLS for scores and profiles.
Require authenticated users to write only their own user_id and prevent client-controlled verified fields.
Move score acceptance to a server-side RPC or edge function that validates a signed run summary or replay evidence where practical.
Add a production release check that records the latest Supabase policy audit status before publishing.

issues.title: Hosted production lacks explicit browser security headers
issues.summary: The live GitHub Pages response did not expose obvious hardening headers such as Content-Security-Policy, frame-ancestors or X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, or HSTS.
evidence: Passive header check on the live production URL showed GitHub Pages defaults and no obvious app-owned hardening headers.
The app is a large inline JS bundle and uses many innerHTML rendering paths, so lack of CSP increases blast radius if any injection bug appears.
proposedPlan: Decide whether to keep GitHub Pages or front it with a host that can set response headers.
Add CSP, frame-ancestors, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, and X-Content-Type-Options where the host permits.
If staying on GitHub Pages, add the strictest feasible meta CSP and document the residual limits.
Track any required unsafe-inline exceptions and remove them as build packaging improves.

issues.title: Production build metadata exposes test pilot emails and internal release context
issues.summary: Production build-info and bundle metadata include non-production test pilot emails and detailed commit/release state. This is not a secret leak by itself, but it gives unnecessary phishing and reconnaissance targets.
evidence: The production bundle includes the same configured test pilot emails.
build-info.json also exposes commit, branch, promoted-from state, build timing, and release-review context.
proposedPlan: Remove non-production test pilot emails from production build-info and production JS.
Keep only public-safe release metadata in production build-info.
Move internal promotion lineage and test account data to private release artifacts.
Add metadata minimization checks to production publish preflight.

issues.title: Production bundle mixes dev, beta, and production account/test logic
issues.summary: Production code contains non-production test account rules, harness bypass concepts, and force-write controls. Even when guarded, this expands public attack surface and makes production security reasoning harder.
evidence: Production bundle includes TEST_ACCOUNT_EMAILS, NON_PRODUCTION_LANE, testAccountEnabled, and force-write harness flag names.
Auth and score behavior branches by release lane inside the public runtime instead of being stripped by build lane.
proposedPlan: Split lane-specific dev/test controls from production runtime at build time.
Keep production account and leaderboard logic minimal and explicit.
Add a production bundle scan for dev/test-only symbols.
Document any intentionally retained diagnostics as read-only support surfaces.
